title: TC001-Filter by category + price range, switch to List view, and open a product detail
description: Verifies users can browse products, apply category and price filters, change view mode, and navigate to a product detail page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /products (use navigate action to http://localhost:3000/products).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/products", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click on category filter 'Women's Fashion' (the 'Women' category) to apply the category filter.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/label[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Women' category filter using the current interactive element index (1905).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/label[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Loading').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Price minimum input ('Min') not found on the page after scrolling and searching.
- Price maximum input ('Max') not found on the page after scrolling and searching.
- Price filter controls are not accessible (no visible slider handles or numeric inputs found), preventing applying the requested price range 0–150.
- The test cannot complete the required filter-and-navigate flow because the price filter UI is missing or not interactable.

title: TC002-Validate product detail page loads after selecting a product from listings
description: Verifies that clicking a product from the listing navigates to a product detail page and shows key product content.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /products (use explicit navigation to http://localhost:3000/products) and then wait for the product listing to appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/products", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the first visible product card (try a likely product-card container element index).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[4]/a/div/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/products/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Add to Cart').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Price').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Clicking a product card on the /products listing did not navigate to a product detail page after two attempts.
- Current URL remained on /products after the click attempts, indicating click handlers on product cards may not be wired.
- Product detail page content is accessible directly at /products/p10 (Add to Cart and Price present), indicating detail pages exist but listing-to-detail navigation is broken.

title: TC003-No-results empty state and Clear Filters restores results
description: Verifies empty state appears when filters return no matches and Clear Filters restores product results.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /products (use the exact path http://localhost:3000/products as requested).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/products", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Wait for a product element to be visible, then select a Category filter (Kids) and a Brand filter (TimeCraft) to attempt to produce an empty result set.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/label[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div/div[3]/div/label[10]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Wait for a product element to be visible, then click the 'Kids' category filter (index 1910) and then click the 'TimeCraft' brand filter (index 1958) to attempt to produce an empty results set.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/label[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'TimeCraft' brand filter (index 4062) to attempt to produce an empty results set, then verify the empty state and clear filters behavior.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[3]/div/label[10]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Clear All Filters' button to clear filters and then verify products reappear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[1]/a/div/div[2]/div/button[1]').wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        raise AssertionError("Required empty-state text 'No products' and/or 'Clear Filters' button not present on the page. Cannot complete the empty-state / Clear Filters verification as specified in the test plan.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Sort by Price: Low to High updates visible ordering
description: Verifies sorting by price low-to-high changes the product ordering in the listing.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /products (use explicit navigate action to http://localhost:3000/products).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/products", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Wait for product elements to be visible, then click the sorting dropdown ('Featured') to open sorting options.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Price: Low to High' option in the sorting dropdown to apply the sort (element index 1388).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert "/products" in frame.url
        price_btn = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/button')
        visible = await price_btn.is_visible()
        assert visible, "Expected text \"Price: Low to High\" to be visible on the page."
        raise AssertionError("Element with text 'Product' is not present on the page (no matching xpath in available elements). Cannot verify product visibility or sorting. Test marked as done.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers

        )

        # Click element

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Open the first product's detail/quick-view so variant controls are visible by clicking the product image (click element index 2001).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Select an available size option (click size button). Immediate action: click size option (index 4626). After that: select color, then click Add to Cart, then verify CartDrawer and confirmation UI.
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[8]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[@id="cart-drawer"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=added').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Subtotal').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Add to Cart button remained disabled/greyed out after selecting size and color, preventing addition to cart.

- CartDrawer did not appear after the Add to Cart attempt.
- No 'added' confirmation text was displayed after the Add to Cart attempt.
- No 'Subtotal' text or cart totals are visible in the UI after the Add to Cart attempt.

title: TC017-Complete checkout from cart through confirmation and verify cart is emptied
description: Verifies the end-to-end checkout flow (shipping → payment → review → place order) and that the cart is cleared after confirmation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the Shopping cart button to open the cart page (use interactive element index 72).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /cart using the explicit path /cart on the current site (http://localhost:3000/cart).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/cart", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/checkout' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Processing...').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Order').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Order Number').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Your cart is empty').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Proceed to Checkout button not found on cart page; cart page shows 'Your cart is empty'.
- Checkout flow cannot be started because there are no items in the cart to proceed with shipping/payment/review steps.
- Order placement and order-number verification could not be performed because checkout pages (shipping/payment/review/place order) are not reachable from the current UI.

title: TC018-Checkout blocks progression when required shipping fields are empty
description: Verifies field-level validation appears on Shipping and the user cannot proceed to Payment with missing required fields.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the Shopping cart button to open the checkout page (prefer clicking a page element rather than direct URL navigation).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Required').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Shipping').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Payment').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Checkout page not reachable: no items in cart and no 'Checkout' or 'Continue to Payment' button present in the cart drawer.
- Field-level validation for Shipping cannot be verified because the checkout form is not accessible.
- Unable to proceed to /checkout via on-page interactions; the required checkout flow is not reachable from the current UI state.

title: TC025-Complete the full 10-step quiz and open a recommended product detail page

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Take Style Quiz' link to navigate to the style quiz page and then verify the 'Start Quiz' text is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Start Quiz').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Analyzing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Recommendations').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/products/' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Take Style Quiz' link to open the style quiz (use element index 113).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /style-quiz (http://localhost:3000/style-quiz).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/style-quiz", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Locate and click the element that starts the quiz (look for a 'Start Quiz' control). If none exists, choose the relevant starter option ('Who are you shopping for?' -> Women's Fashion) to begin.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Next Step' button to advance to the first image-selection step.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Scroll slightly if needed and click the 'Modern Minimalist' aesthetic option to advance the quiz (index 1698).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Next Step' button to advance to the image-selection flow so image options become visible (advance to first image-selection step).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the color palette option 'Neutrals (Black, White, Beige, Gray)' (index 1733) to answer the current question so the quiz can advance toward the image-selection flow.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Analyzing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Featured picks').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/products/' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Toggle between Grid and List view updates layout without page reload
description: Verifies users can toggle view modes and the product presentation changes while staying on the same listing page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /products using exact path http://localhost:3000/products (per test instruction).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/products", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Wait for product elements to be visible and then click the 'List' view toggle (use element index 1408). After clicking, wait for the view to update so the next assertion can be made.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Grid' view toggle (index 1401), wait for the view to update, then finish by reporting whether the presentation changed and the URL remained on /products.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Assertions for product listing view toggles and URL
        await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[1]/a').wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert "/products" in frame.url
        await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').is_visible()
        await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[1]').wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[1]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[1]').is_visible()
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
